response.justification: The candidate is a strong and versatile senior software developer with a solid track record in web, full-stack, and some systems-level work, but the profile is a poor match for this role. The position requires native-level Japanese proficiency, deep C/UNIX development, Linux kernel source-code modification experience, and embedded LAN-switch security expertise (MACsec, 802.1X, L2/L3 networking) — none of which appear on the resume. His C/C++ experience is limited to one short stint, and his recent career centers on TypeScript/Node.js product work rather than embedded firmware or network security.
strengths: Broad, long-tenured software engineering experience across web, mobile, desktop, and some embedded contexts
Systems-level exposure: low-latency DLang services, C/C++ at National Instruments, Linux proficiency
Strong engineering practices — TDD, CI/CD, testing tooling, and open-source contributions
concerns: No indication of Japanese language proficiency, a hard requirement for the role
No embedded networking or security-domain experience (MACsec, 802.1X, switch firmware, kernel modification)
C for UNIX experience is minimal and dated; recent work is web/full-stack TypeScript, far from firmware development

key_factors.factor: Strong general engineering fundamentals and systems exposure
